title: Malta Considers Regulating Some DeFi Under MiCA
summary: Malta's financial regulator is exploring whether parts of decentralized finance (DeFi) should fall under the EU's MiCA regulations. The MFSA is seeking feedback on how to assess decentralization, treating it as a spectrum rather than a binary concept.
body:
Malta's Financial Services Authority (MFSA) is considering bringing parts of decentralized finance (DeFi) under the European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework. The regulator is seeking public feedback on how to approach the decentralization of DeFi projects, suggesting that decentralization should be viewed as a spectrum rather than an absolute concept.

The MFSA's consultation paper, published on June 18, 2026, aims to gather insights on how to regulate DeFi projects that may not be fully decentralized. This move comes as regulators worldwide grapple with the challenges of applying traditional financial regulations to the rapidly evolving DeFi space. The paper highlights the need for a nuanced approach, considering the varying degrees of decentralization among different DeFi protocols.

This development is significant for everyday crypto users as it could impact the accessibility and operation of DeFi platforms. If parts of DeFi are brought under MiCA, users may experience changes in compliance requirements, operational transparency, and potentially even the types of services offered. The MFSA's approach could set a precedent for other regulators, influencing how DeFi is treated globally.

For those interested in the future of DeFi regulation, keeping an eye on the MFSA's consultation process and the feedback received will be crucial. The regulator's final decisions could shape the regulatory landscape for DeFi in the EU and beyond. Users and stakeholders are encouraged to participate in the consultation to voice their opinions and concerns.
twitterThread: Malta's regulator is considering bringing parts of DeFi under EU's MiCA rules. The key question: is decentralization a spectrum or binary?
The MFSA is seeking feedback on how to assess decentralization in DeFi, treating it as a spectrum. This could impact how DeFi platforms operate globally.

If you use or invest in DeFi, pay attention to this consultation. Your input could shape future regulations. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-18-malta-considers-regulating-some-defi-under-mica
